assessment_items.question_content: **An inscription in the Lindisfarne Gospels tells us that Bishop Eadfrith created the book to honor [[☃ dropdown 1]] and [[☃ dropdown 2]], whose relics were at Lindisfarne Priory.**
hints:

assessment_items.question_content:
**What detail of Luke's portrait page in the Lindisfarne Gospels suggests a Classical influence on Hiberno-Saxon art?**  

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0fc7c89048a22a50e463fb3386cc0f98.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**In contrast to the decorative patterning of the cross-carpet pages, this portrait page depicts Luke in robes that recall the Classical poses of philosophers in Roman and Greek art.  **

**[Read this essay to review.](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/anglo-saxon-england/a/the-lindisfarne-gospels)**

choices.content: the interlaced patterns of the border
the composite view of the calf

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which term would best apply to this page from the Lindisfarne Gospels?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1a66a143269868b80f6a29ad15857896.jpg) 


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **A codex is a type of early book made by binding together multiple sheets of paper, vellum, or other material used for writing.  **

**Incipit pages like the one below  include the first few words of a poem or text, which were often heavily illuminated in medieval manuscripts. **

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/64b77c431b4bffe955cb27b819284c71.jpg) 
Mark incipit page, the Lindisfarne Gospels. (The British Library, Cotton MS Nero D.iv,)

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0b71cace4cbf357c89a1ab7f3cea7c21.jpg) Lindisfarne Gospel, Luke cross-carpet page, f 138 v (British Museum)

Decorated pages, like this one, appear at the beginning of each Gospel in the Lindisfarne Gospels.  They are known as Carpet Pages because they look like oriental rugs.  Prayer mats were known in Northumbria at this time, as well as in eastern Christian and Islamic lands. Their resemblance may reflect a similar function to prepare the worshipper for the Gospel message.

**[Read this essay to review.](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/anglo-saxon-england/a/the-lindisfarne-gospels)**
